jokes.text: Mr. Professor, fuel status. If I'm reading this correctly, we're underwater. You're not reading it correctly.
jokes.setup: Leela asks for fuel status in professional captain voice
jokes.punchline: Professor's incompetent reading of instruments gets dry correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Professor's scientific incompetence played straight against Leela's competence. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Medical Officer Zoidberg, are you reaching for the cake? Affirmative, Captain. Ow! Stick to fish flakes, you overgrown sea monkey.
jokes.setup: Formal military protocol applied to cake protection
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg gets physically stopped, insulted as sea monkey
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg food obsession bit. 'Overgrown sea monkey' is a nice insult but predictable setup.

jokes.text: Is that crab Rangoon? No, he's Dr. Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Guest mistakes character for appetizer
jokes.punchline: Deadpan correction that Zoidberg is crew, not food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - entire joke in 7 words. Works because Zoidberg does look like seafood. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Here, place my lead-lined overcape in the Closet of Heavy Things. [groans] I love my job.
jokes.setup: Alien royalty has absurdly heavy formal wear
jokes.punchline: Crew member struggles with weight, sarcastically loves job
jokes.explanation: 'Closet of Heavy Things' is nice worldbuilding humor. Physical comedy lands but sarcasm is predictable.

jokes.text: Wrong! Ndnd got them from an inspirational placemat. It was a quality placemat.
jokes.setup: Wedding vows should be personal and meaningful
jokes.punchline: Borrowed from restaurant placemat, defended as 'quality'
jokes.explanation: Subverts romantic expectations beautifully. 'Quality placemat' defense makes it even funnier. Original concept.

jokes.text: Your dress covered in the blood of three B'dnkian warriors. You took me in your arms and ate the morsel of B'dnkian entrail lodged in my cleavage. It was delicious.
jokes.setup: Reading romantic wedding vows about their first meeting
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely violent and cannibalistic 'romantic' memory
jokes.explanation: Perfect alien romance parody - takes romantic tropes and makes them horrifying. Character-specific humor that only works for these aliens.

jokes.text: Hey, you got any B'dnkian entrail on that tray? No, but we have lettuce-wrapped cucumbers, which are even grosser.
jokes.setup: Guest wants the gross alien food just mentioned
jokes.punchline: Earth vegetables somehow grosser than alien entrails
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that reverses expectations about what's gross. Quick callback timing works well.

jokes.text: Can you stop eating for one second? If you want me to not eat, then you should have done the cooking.
jokes.setup: Someone complains about Zoidberg eating during ceremony
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's logic: complaint invalid because they didn't cook
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg logic - completely wrong but delivered with confidence. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, snap.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg delivers surprisingly good comeback
jokes.punchline: Someone genuinely impressed by his burn
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that acknowledges Zoidberg's rare win. Economy is good but otherwise unremarkable.

jokes.text: Having completed the sacred vows, the groom will now crush the skull of his father-in-law, if it please the Lord? Yeah, I love that kind of stuff.
jokes.setup: Formal wedding ceremony language about sacred traditions
jokes.punchline: Tradition is skull-crushing, groom enthusiastically agrees
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of wedding ceremony expectations. Lrrr's casual enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Keep it clean. My father's crushed skull is here. Intelligent, loyal, brave. A great leader. But enough about me.
jokes.setup: Best man giving speech about groom's good qualities
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was describing himself, not the groom
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection structure executed well. Callback to skull-crushing adds context.

jokes.text: Uh, nobody invited the help. Nobody invited me to kick your ass either. Yet here we are.
jokes.setup: Snooty guest dismisses working crew as uninvited help
jokes.punchline: Crew member threatens violence using same logical structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - mirrors the setup's logic perfectly. 'Yet here we are' is perfectly delivered. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Ah. That takes care of the dishes. You want me to get started on the floors, Hermes? Or should I scrub down Dr. Zoidberg? Scrub-a-dub-dub.
jokes.setup: Frank asking about cleaning priorities after washing dishes
jokes.punchline: Suggests cleaning Zoidberg like an object, Zoidberg agrees
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor treating Zoidberg as cleanable object. His enthusiastic agreement sells it.

jokes.text: How are you so energetic? Oh, I'll never get tired of this job. I have the best life ever. That's our Fry. It sure is. It most definitely is.
jokes.setup: Frank being extremely enthusiastic about menial temp job
jokes.punchline: Crew's increasingly forced agreement that this is 'typical Fry'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the identity swap mystery with forced responses. Works as setup but not a strong standalone joke.

jokes.text: How will we stay in business without our ace delivery boy? Extremely easily.
jokes.setup: Professor worries about losing their essential delivery boy
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself - they don't need Fry at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment and structure. Professor's absent-minded reversal is economically delivered.

jokes.text: Come on, let's get a temp dimwit to replace our permanent one.
jokes.setup: Need to hire temporary replacement worker
jokes.punchline: Calls Fry permanent dimwit, temp will be dimwit too
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay on temp/permanent. Professor's casual cruelty toward Fry is in character.

jokes.text: I'm sorry to keep you waiting. I don't know what happened to our office manager, Evans. Is that him? Maybe.
jokes.setup: Temp agency apologizes for missing office manager
jokes.punchline: Manager might be the unidentifiable corpse/mess in office
jokes.explanation: Dark visual gag about workplace safety. 'Maybe' is perfectly casual response to possible death.

jokes.text: Well, let's see. It's a thankless job. He'll never be thanked. Any skills? Absolutely not. I'll need to berate him for being incompetent.
jokes.setup: Describing job requirements for temp worker
jokes.punchline: Escalates from thankless to actively abusive treatment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. Professor's honest cruelty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Then I've got just the schlep for you. Frank, you're going to be working at Planet Express for two weeks. Lucky me.
jokes.setup: Agency has perfect match for terrible job described
jokes.punchline: Frank's sarcastic response to 'lucky' assignment
jokes.explanation: Frank's deadpan sarcasm establishes his character. 'Schlep' is good word choice.

jokes.text: Hi, Frank. Tell us about yourself. I have no characteristics, and people forget me as soon as I leave the room. And, sometimes, before that.
jokes.setup: Standard new employee introduction request
jokes.punchline: Frank describes his forgettability, escalates to being forgotten while present
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character establishment. Escalation from 'leave room' to 'before that' is perfect. Sets up entire episode premise.

jokes.text: Pfft. That's ridiculous. Um...
jokes.setup: Amy dismisses Frank's claim about being forgettable
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgets what she was going to say
jokes.explanation: Immediate proof of Frank's claim through Amy's memory lapse. Perfect timing and economy.

jokes.text: Frank. Is this my cubicle? No, that's an empty space between three stacks of boxes. Well, I guess I'll stand in there.
jokes.setup: Frank asks about workspace assignment
jokes.punchline: Assigned to non-space between boxes, accepts it resignedly
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about temp worker treatment. Frank's resignation to terrible conditions is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I don't have time for that. Temping is a full-time job.
jokes.setup: Suggestion Frank could easily travel to Paris for 5 cents
jokes.punchline: Claims temp work too demanding for any personal life
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about temp work paradox. Frank's dedication to terrible jobs is absurd.

jokes.text: Those are beautiful. Thanks a lot, uh, I want to say, Dave. Frank. I'm the temp. I've been here two weeks, the best two weeks of my life.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to thank Frank but can't remember his name
jokes.punchline: Guesses wrong name, Frank corrects and reveals this as life's peak
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Frank's forgettability theme. His genuine happiness at terrible job is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Ugh, he's so enthusiastic. I can't stand his toxic positivity. He likes everyone, even me. It's pathetic.
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about Frank's positive attitude
jokes.punchline: Specifically bothered that someone likes him - reveals his self-hatred
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - hates positivity, especially when directed at him. 'Toxic positivity' is modern term used well.

jokes.text: Wow. Fry's not even here, and you remember his name? He must be quite a guy.
jokes.setup: Crew mentions Fry by name while forgetting Frank's
jokes.punchline: Frank notes the irony - absent person remembered, present person forgotten
jokes.explanation: Smart meta observation about his own situation. Frank's resigned amazement at the irony is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Who the hell is he?
jokes.setup: Frank has been working there for weeks, interacting with everyone
jokes.punchline: Professor has no memory of him whatsoever
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to forgettability theme. Professor's complete bafflement after two weeks is absurd.

jokes.text: You're throwing out all the liberry books? Look, nobody's checked a book out since 2029. It was this one. 'A Novelization of the TikTok Video, Peanut Butter-Eating Dog Lip Syncs to Olivia Rodrigo'
jokes.setup: All library books being discarded due to no use
jokes.punchline: Last book checked out was adaptation of adaptation of social media content
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of content degradation - book of TikTok video is peak absurdity. Very quotable title.

jokes.text: Ooh, I'd watch that. It's a book. You'd read it. [scoffs] No, I wouldn't.
jokes.setup: Bender expresses interest in the ridiculous book title
jokes.punchline: Reminded it's a book, immediately loses all interest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - interested until learning effort required. Structure and economy are excellent.

jokes.text: It's been illegal to dump hazardous waste in the ocean for over a year now.
jokes.setup: Need to dispose of books responsibly
jokes.punchline: Ocean dumping only recently became illegal (implying it was legal before)
jokes.explanation: Good environmental satire - 'over a year' suggests recent change. Books as hazardous waste is nice touch.

jokes.text: Ew, I got book on me! It's so weird that reading used to be a thing.
jokes.setup: Amy disgusted by contact with books
jokes.punchline: Treats reading as bizarre historical practice
jokes.explanation: Good future satire - books as contamination, reading as ancient weirdness. 'Book on me' like toxic substance.

jokes.text: Let's get out of here before some dork says trashing books is a travesty. That's the last of them. Hey, guys? Guys, the ship is abandoning us! Guys? Oh.
jokes.setup: Leela wants to leave before anyone objects to book destruction
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately expresses that exact sentiment, gets abandoned
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony - Leela's prediction immediately comes true. Frank's isolated 'Oh.' is perfect.

jokes.text: Only a delivery to book hell. We're just lucky we made it back without reading anything. We did all make it back, right? Well, let's see. Here's me, Bender, and some other people. We're good.
jokes.setup: Leela checking if everyone returned from dangerous mission
jokes.punchline: Counts 'some other people' instead of names, misses Frank entirely
jokes.explanation: Reinforces forgettability theme through imprecise head count. Sets up 23-year abandonment.

jokes.text: Five, six, seven, eight. All eight crew members present and accounted for. Yep. Mmm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Professor doing official head count after mission
jokes.punchline: Counts wrong number but declares everyone present anyway
jokes.explanation: Professor's incompetent counting completes the abandonment setup. His confident confirmation despite error is character-perfect.

jokes.text: I can get through this. I'm a temp, damn it. I'm sure they'll be back soon.
jokes.setup: Frank stranded alone on desolate planet
jokes.punchline: Uses temp worker identity as source of strength, maintains false hope
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - temp worker pride as survival mechanism. 'Damn it' adds determination to hopeless situation.

jokes.text: After this wedding cake delivery, you should take a vacation. When's the last time you took one? Twenty three years ago, after that horrific snu-snu incident.
jokes.setup: Suggestion Fry needs vacation, asking about his last one
jokes.punchline: Exact timeframe that coincides with Frank's abandonment
jokes.explanation: Good setup for revelation - timing coincidence will expose the truth. Callback to Amazonia.

jokes.text: Or did we? Oh, right. The temp! What's-his-name! He had a name? Even I looked down on him.
jokes.setup: Crew slowly remembering they hired a temp
jokes.punchline: Can't remember name, Bender admits to looking down on already-forgotten person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Bender - even he had hierarchy with Frank at bottom. Escalating memory failure.

jokes.text: We'll just swing by on the way to the wedding and pick up his corpse.
jokes.setup: Professor suggests checking on abandoned Frank
jokes.punchline: Assumes he's dead, treats corpse retrieval as casual errand
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and casual about death. 'Pick up his corpse' like grocery shopping. Perfect Professor callousness.

jokes.text: You came back. I... I thought you'd forgotten about me. Oh, no. We could never forget... Uh... Frank! Of course! Sounds right.
jokes.setup: Frank emotional about crew's return after 23 years
jokes.punchline: Crew still can't remember his name, has to be reminded
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - even after 23 years and return mission, still forgotten. Emotional/comedic contrast works well.

jokes.text: What did you eat? Books. Book sandwiches, book soup, scrambled books with fly larvae. Ew, books!
jokes.setup: Question about Frank's survival diet over 23 years
jokes.punchline: Ate books prepared various ways, Amy more disgusted by books than larvae
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of book preparation methods. Amy's priorities (books worse than larvae) callback to earlier disgust.

jokes.text: There's plenty of soap and toothpaste in Fry's cubby. He never uses them.
jokes.setup: Directing Frank to hygiene supplies for cleanup
jokes.punchline: Fry's complete lack of personal hygiene maintenance
jokes.explanation: Standard Fry gross-out humor. In character but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: It's just as itchy as I dreamed it would be.
jokes.setup: Frank trying on clean clothes after 23 years
jokes.punchline: Had specific dreams about clothing texture, they're accurate
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist line - dreaming about clothing texture during survival. Shows Frank's longing for normal life.

jokes.text: Finally, a use for the Bible.
jokes.setup: Bible being used for some practical purpose
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses religious text as only useful for mundane task
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender atheism/pragmatism. Economy is excellent but concept is familiar for his character.

jokes.text: Why hast thou forsaken me?
jokes.setup: Bible being used inappropriately by Bender
jokes.punchline: Bible itself quotes Jesus's crucifixion words about abandonment
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke - Bible commenting on its own mistreatment. Biblical reference fits context of abandonment.

jokes.text: There's a fresh episode of Mandarin Orange is the New Chicken.
jokes.setup: Frank suggesting they watch streaming content together
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Orange is the New Black' but about food/dinners
jokes.explanation: Decent parody title but not exceptional. Shows future evolution of streaming content names.

jokes.text: This twice-flushed pork is delicious.
jokes.setup: Family dinner with Leela's parents
jokes.punchline: Food described in terms suggesting toilet/sewage processing
jokes.explanation: Decent gross-out humor for sewer mutants. 'Twice-flushed' is economical but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: While during this same period, we're using twice as much soap in the men's room. I have noticed less body odor. Definitely. Way less.
jokes.setup: Professor noting increased soap usage as evidence of changes
jokes.punchline: Everyone confirms massive improvement in hygiene standards
jokes.explanation: Functional setup for identity reveal but not particularly funny on its own. Callback works structurally.

jokes.text: He didn't have a name, poor guy. Frank. His name was Frank. Right. Whatever happened to him? Like I care. I don't.
jokes.setup: Crew trying to remember abandoned temp worker
jokes.punchline: Bender's obvious overcompensation about not caring
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Bender - protests too much about not caring. Sets up his later guilt.

jokes.text: And no sign of Frank. Let's just go bowling. Oh, thank God you're here. You're just in time to see my beard.
jokes.setup: Crew arrives expecting Frank to be dead
jokes.punchline: Frank alive and excited to show off 23-year beard growth
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of death expectation. Frank's priorities (showing beard) after 23 years of isolation is character-perfect.

jokes.text: You're not Fry. That's Fry. Yes. Fry.
jokes.setup: Real Fry trying to prove his identity
jokes.punchline: Frank's simple confirmation while obviously being imposter
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery from Frank. His simple 'Yes. Fry.' while obviously not being Fry is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: I do sense a familiar dopiness.
jokes.setup: Leela examining the bearded Fry's claims
jokes.punchline: Recognizes Fry's essential stupidity despite appearance changes
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation - Fry's stupidity as identifying characteristic. 'Familiar dopiness' is economical and accurate.

jokes.text: That could be me with anyone stupid. I have a picture with everybody.
jokes.setup: Frank shows photo evidence of friendship with Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses it, admits to photographing with all stupid people
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - has photos with everyone, so photos prove nothing. Self-aware about his social habits.

jokes.text: 'The Art of Klang Foo.' I must have read this 1,000 times. And The Return of The King? That one, I didn't read. It's the third book of the trilogy, and I've never read the first two. I didn't want to spoil it.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his reading material during 23-year isolation
jokes.punchline: Wouldn't read Lord of the Rings conclusion to avoid spoilers despite isolation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - maintaining spoiler discipline during decades of isolation. Perfect character consistency for Frank's rule-following nature.

jokes.text: Because you have such a great life! I've had so many temp jobs. But working at Planet Express was the one time in my life I was truly happy.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining motivation for identity theft
jokes.punchline: Planet Express job was peak happiness despite being objectively terrible
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about how terrible Frank's life has been. Makes Planet Express job seem good by comparison. Strong character motivation.

jokes.text: Whoa. Bleak.
jokes.setup: Frank's revelation about his sad life circumstances
jokes.punchline: Fry's understated response to genuinely depressing information
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word captures the dark reality. Fry's casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I should have paid more attention that week I was a temp astronaut!
jokes.setup: Frank struggling to pilot the spacecraft
jokes.punchline: References his previous temp job as astronaut, admits to not paying attention
jokes.explanation: Great callback to temp work variety. 'Temp astronaut' is perfect absurdity - specialized job as temporary work.

jokes.text: Now what? I have an idea, but I have to warn you, it's completely brilliant.
jokes.setup: Crew trapped, asking for solutions to escape
jokes.punchline: Bender's characteristic self-aggrandizement about his own ideas
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender ego moment. Structure works well with false modesty leading to boasting.

jokes.text: How much radiation? Oh, not much. Like having an affair with Marie Curie.
jokes.setup: Question about radiation exposure from escape plan
jokes.punchline: Compares to affair with famous radiation researcher, implies lethal dose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical reference joke. Double meaning of 'affair' (romantic/radiation exposure). Highly quotable and clever.

jokes.text: What's that for? I think I was gonna throw it at somebody. Who? I can't remember.
jokes.setup: Frank holding weapon he prepared, asked about its purpose
jokes.punchline: Forgets his own plan despite just making the weapon
jokes.explanation: Good callback to memory theme. Frank's confusion about his own actions fits the absurdist resolution.

jokes.text: I don't need a vacation. I just had six weeks off. You did? I could have sworn you were here, but I'm happy to dock your pay.
jokes.setup: Fry claims he doesn't need vacation because he had time off
jokes.punchline: Professor can't remember but will still dock pay for imaginary vacation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character moment - confused but still looking to exploit workers. Ironic resolution to identity swap.
